title: it is not possible to filter a delta lake table by partition key when using parquet v3
description:
### Company or project name
_No response_
### Describe what's wrong
When creating a `DeltaLake` with `input_format_parquet_use_native_reader_v3 = 1` it is not possible to query it by partition key. It returns the error "Code: 10. DB::Exception: Not found column <partition_name>: in block ...."
This does not reproduce when creating a table with `input_format_parquet_use_native_reader_v3 = 0`.
### Does it reproduce on the most recent release?
Yes
### How to reproduce
1. run `ClickHouse local version 25.11.1.1706 (official build).`
2. `CREATE TABLE tableee ENGINE = DeltaLake(...) SETTINGS input_format_parquet_use_native_reader_v3 = 1;`
Run a query that filters by partition key:
`SELECT * FROM tableee WHERE <partition_name> = <val>`
3. Error
### Expected behavior
_No response_
### Error message and/or stacktrace
_No response_
### Additional context
_No response_
bug_features_seed: CREATE TABLE tableee (id INT UNSIGNED, partition_name VARCHAR(255), value FLOAT) ENGINE = InnoDB;
INSERT INTO tableee (id, partition_name, value) VALUES (1, 'part1', 10.5);
INSERT INTO tableee (id, partition_name, value) VALUES (2, 'part2', NULL);
SELECT * FROM tableee WHERE partition_name IS NULL;
key_bug_features: Filtering DeltaLake table by partition key fails with parquet v3
